questions.question: Mount Nemrut, recently seen in the news, is located in which country?
questions.explanation: NASA’s International Space Station captured an unusual crater on Mount Nemrut, one of the Eastern Taurus’ highest peaks in southeastern Türkiye. Named after King Nimrod, it stands 9,672 feet tall at the Arabian-Eurasian tectonic plates’ intersection. This geologically young stratovolcano last erupted in 1650 C.E. Additionally, it hosts the Hiero Theseion, built by Hellenistic King Antiochos I, and is a UNESCO World Cultural Heritage site and a prominent National Park in Türkiye.

questions.question: GSAP SKILLS PLATFORM, recently seen in the news, is an initiative of which organization?

options.label: B
options.text: International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN)

answer.label: B
answer.text: International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN)

questions.explanation: The GSAP SKILLS Platform, launched at the Fourth meeting of the Subsidiary Body on Implementation, Convention on Biological Diversity. It aims to bolster global species conservation efforts. Managed by IUCN, it connects decision-makers, practitioners, and experts, providing real-time updates and technical resources. Supported by the Ministry of Environment, Republic of Korea, and resources from the Tech4Nature Initiative by IUCN and Huawei, it facilitates collaboration and implements biodiversity targets with actionable interventions and tools.

questions.question: What is ‘Stellaria mcclintockiae’, recently seen in news?
questions.explanation: A new plant species, Stellaria mcclintockiae, was discovered on the muddy slopes of Kerala’s Nelliyampathy hills, at elevations of 1,250-1,400 meters. This annual herb, growing up to 15 cm tall, belongs to the genus Stellaria (family Caryophyllaceae). It features distinct petals, pollen morphology, bracts, sepals, and seed architecture. As the first Stellaria species found in South India, researchers recommend it be classified as critically endangered.

questions.question: Which two institutions recently signed an MoU to strengthen cybersecurity in India?

options.label: A
options.text: Common Services Centers (CSC) & United Service Institution (USI)

answer.label: A
answer.text: Common Services Centers (CSC) & United Service Institution (USI)

questions.explanation: As cyber threats increase globally, Common Services Centers (CSC) play a crucial role in disseminating cybersecurity knowledge. At the CSC Cyber Security Conclave 2024, MeitY Secretary S Krishnan emphasized the importance of user behavior in security. CSC and the United Service Institution (USI) signed an MoU to enhance cybersecurity. CSC’s initiatives include a cyber think tank and the ‘Cyber Rakshak’ program, training women as cybersecurity ambassadors.

questions.question: Virupaksha Temple, recently seen in the news, is located in which state?
questions.explanation: A portion of Hampi’s Virupaksha Temple in Karnataka, specifically the ‘saalu mantap’ pavilion, collapsed due to torrential rains. Dedicated to Lord Virupaksha, a form of Shiva, the temple is part of the UNESCO World Heritage Site at Hampi, the former capital of the Vijayanagara kingdom. It showcases Dravida temple architecture with grand gopuras, Vimana, intricate carvings, and pillared halls.

questions.question: What is ‘Nephrotic syndrome’, recently seen in news?
questions.explanation: Scientists have discovered that anti-nephrin autoantibodies can be used as biomarkers for monitoring the progression of nephrotic syndrome. This kidney disorder leads to excessive protein loss from the blood into the urine, causing damage to the glomeruli. The glomeruli are tiny blood vessels in the kidneys that filter waste and excess water. Nephrotic syndrome can be caused by various disorders, including diabetes and lupus.

questions.question: Which institute recently launched the ‘Electric Tiller’?

options.label: C
options.text: CSIR-Central Institute of Mining and Fuel Research

questions.explanation: CSIR-Central Mechanical Engineering Research launched an Electric Tiller for small farmers, prioritizing user comfort and eco-sustainability. Features include enhanced torque, electronic controls, and versatile charging options like AC and Solar DC. It produces zero exhaust emissions, supporting India’s commitment to eco-friendly farming. Compatible with various attachments, it minimizes operator fatigue, boosts productivity, and has the potential to reduce operational costs by up to 85%. Tilling improves soil quality and promotes healthy crop growth.

questions.question: Irrawaddy River, recently seen in news, is located in which country?
questions.explanation: Myanmar’s military regime has formed a body to evaluate hydropower projects on the Irrawaddy River, potentially reviving the controversial Myitsone Dam. Amid worsening power shortages since the 2021 coup, the regime aims to address these issues and boost domestic production.

questions.question: Kumaon Himalayas, recently seen in the news, lies between which rivers?
questions.explanation: The Supreme Court has halted a 90-acre project in the ecologically fragile and seismic-prone lower Himalayan ranges of Uttarakhand’s Kumaon hills. This region lies between the Sutlej and Kali Rivers and includes the Siwalik Range and Great Himalayas. It features peaks like Nanda Devi and Kamet, lakes such as Naini and Bhimtal, and hill stations like Nainital. It is home to indigenous tribes including Tharus and Bhutiyas.

questions.question: Which space organization has recently launched a tiny satellite to measure heat lost from Earth’s poles?
questions.explanation: NASA recently launched one of two climate satellites to study heat emissions at Earth’s poles, named PREFIRE (Polar Radiant Energy in the Far-InfraRed Experiment). This mission is vital for analyzing Earth’s energy budget, which determines its temperature and climate. Currently, there’s no way to measure far-infrared radiation emitted from the Arctic and Antarctica, leaving a gap in understanding the planet’s energy balance. The second satellite will be launched soon to address this gap.
